behavior_summary: U8P_NSLAMPTP implements a very small 'U8P_NSLAMPTP::look' (examine) process for an in-game object. When the player inspects the object the process emits a short descriptive bark "lamp " and then suspends/waits.

There are no menu interactions, flag reads/writes, external API calls, or branching. The routine's sole gameplay effect is to present the description text and pause until the engine advances the process.
notable_control_flow: Single-entry process U8P_NSLAMPTP::look is triggered by the engine's U8P_NSLAMPTP::look/examine event and runs linearly: show bark then suspend.
No conditional branches, loops, or inter-process calls are present in the disassembly output.
open_questions: Does the trailing space in the bark text "lamp " reflect concatenation with additional text at runtime, or is it an artifact/typo in the data?
What exact engine event advances or resumes the suspended U8P_NSLAMPTP::look process (player input, timed callback, or engine-driven UI dismissal)? This is not explicit in the script.
Is this object instance ever referenced by other scripts or flagged for special behavior elsewhere (no calls/flags are present here so linkage is unknown).

main_function.name: U8P_NSLAMPTP::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short descriptive text shown when the player 'looks at' or inspects the object; then pause/suspend the process until the engine advances.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the game engine's U8P_NSLAMPTP::look/examine event for this object (process type 0x0208).
Likely triggered when the player uses the 'U8P_NSLAMPTP::look' or 'examine' verb on the object in the world or inventory UI.

function_map.name: U8P_NSLAMPTP::look
function_map.role: Handle the object's examine/U8P_NSLAMPTP::look action: display a short description and suspend.
